description: Discrete Riemannian-embedding-stress series across seven lattice sizes. The geometric coherence c_g(N) is the canonical Kruskal-Shepard MDS coherence score, c_g = 1 / (1 + stress), where 'stress' is the normalized embedding stress of the lattice distance matrix in the smallest non-trivial Euclidean target dimension d_target = min(3, N-1). The complementary quantity sigma(N) := 1 - c_g(N) = stress / (1 + stress) is the obstruction to expressing the discrete distance matrix as inner products of a Riemannian metric on a smooth 3-manifold; it is the discrete macroscopic-metric-quality deficit. Distilled from the bundled A2-extension run set (results_a2_real/ and results_a2_real_p[3-6]_extension/) of the canonical pipeline.

definitions.geometric_coherence: c_g(N) = 1 / (1 + stress(N)), Kruskal-Shepard MDS coherence in target dimension min(3, N-1).
definitions.einstein_metric_stress: sigma(N) = 1 - c_g(N) = stress(N) / (1 + stress(N)). Vanishing sigma is the obstruction-free condition for extracting a Riemannian metric on a smooth 3-manifold from the lattice distance matrix; the Einstein equations are exact only when sigma -> 0.
definitions.relation_to_tensor_closure_gap: sigma(N) tracks the macroscopic tensor-closure deficit reported by the canonical multi-N pipeline (companion multi-anchor extension run), with regime-by-regime agreement to ~0.02 in absolute units. sigma(N) is the more direct definition; the canonical tensor-closure gap is a closely-related composite of the same MDS-stress geometry.
definitions.relation_to_einstein_identity_gap: sigma(N) is NOT the Einstein-identity gap ||G_munu - 8 pi G T_munu|| / ||G_munu||; the latter is a separate, narrower quantity that is bundled at the two canonical regimes only, and that DOES converge under Richardson extrapolation. sigma(N) is the broader discrete-Riemannian-embedding axis that characterizes whether a Riemannian metric exists at all on the lattice; it does not vanish at the available lattice sizes.

framing: Across seven lattice sizes spanning a factor of 35 in N, sigma(N) lies in [0.259, 0.337] with a non-monotonic profile that bottoms out near N approximately 4000 and recovers slowly at larger N. The minimum coherence c_g approximately 0.66 corresponds to ~34% obstruction to a clean Euclidean 3-embedding; the lattice has robust but imperfect geometric coherence in this range. Combined with the two-point Einstein-identity-gap Richardson result (which DOES converge), this gives a complementary picture: the Einstein equations close to <5% on the narrow Einstein-identity-gap axis, while the broader Riemannian-embedding axis retains a finite-resolution stress floor at the available lattice sizes.
